keywords.description: package - Declares the package name. Every Go file must start with a package declaration.
import - Imports packages for use in the current file. Can import multiple packages in parentheses.
func - Declares a function with optional parameters and return values. Functions are first-class citizens in Go.
keywords.example: func add(a int, b int) int { return a + b }

keywords.description: var - Declares variables with explicit types. Can declare multiple variables at once.
keywords.example: var name string = "Alice"; var x, y int

keywords.description: const - Declares constants that cannot be changed. Evaluated at compile time.
type - Defines new types including structs, interfaces, and type aliases.
keywords.example: type Person struct { Name string; Age int }

keywords.description: struct - Defines a composite type that groups together fields. Foundation of data modeling in Go.
keywords.example: type User struct { ID int; Name string; Email string }

keywords.description: interface - Defines a contract of methods. Go's way of achieving polymorphism.
keywords.example: type Reader interface { Read(p []byte) (n int, err error) }

keywords.description: if - Conditional statement. Can include initialization before condition.
keywords.example: if x > 0 { }; if err := doSomething(); err != nil { }

keywords.description: else - Alternative execution path when if condition is false.
for - Go's only looping construct. Can be used as while, infinite loop, or traditional for loop.
keywords.example: for i := 0; i < 10; i++ { }; for condition { }; for { }

keywords.description: range - Iterates over slices, arrays, maps, channels, or strings in for loops.
keywords.example: for i, v := range slice { }; for key, value := range map { }

keywords.description: switch - Multi-way conditional. Cases don't fall through by default in Go.
case - Branch in switch or select statement. Can match multiple values.
default - Default case in switch or select when no other case matches.
break - Exits the current loop or switch statement.
continue - Skips to next iteration of the loop.
keywords.example: for i := 0; i < 10; i++ { if skip { continue } }

keywords.description: return - Returns values from a function. Go supports multiple return values.
keywords.example: return result; return value, err; return

keywords.description: defer - Defers execution of a function until surrounding function returns. Used for cleanup.
go - Launches a goroutine (concurrent function execution). Core of Go's concurrency model.
keywords.example: go processData(); go func() { /* anonymous goroutine */ }()

keywords.description: chan - Declares a channel type for goroutine communication. Can be buffered or unbuffered.
keywords.example: var ch chan int; ch := make(chan string, 10)

keywords.description: select - Waits on multiple channel operations. Like switch but for channels.
keywords.example: select { case msg := <-ch1: ; case ch2 <- value: ; default: }

keywords.description: make - Allocates and initializes slices, maps, and channels. Returns initialized value.
new - Allocates memory and returns pointer. Zeroes the memory but doesn't initialize.
append - Appends elements to a slice, growing it if necessary. Returns the updated slice.
keywords.example: slice = append(slice, element); slice = append(slice, elem1, elem2)

keywords.description: len - Returns the length of strings, arrays, slices, maps, and channels.
cap - Returns the capacity of slices, arrays, and channels.
copy - Copies elements from source slice to destination slice. Returns number of elements copied.
delete - Removes a key-value pair from a map.
close - Closes a channel, signaling no more values will be sent.
panic - Stops normal execution and begins panicking. Used for unrecoverable errors.
recover - Regains control of a panicking goroutine. Must be called in deferred function.
keywords.example: defer func() { if r := recover(); r != nil { } }()

keywords.description: error - Built-in interface for error handling. Has single Error() string method.
nil - Zero value for pointers, interfaces, maps, slices, channels, and function types.
keywords.example: var p *int = nil; if err != nil { }

keywords.description: true - Boolean constant representing true.
false - Boolean constant representing false.
iota - Constant generator that increments in const declarations. Resets to 0 in each const block.
keywords.example: const ( A = iota; B; C ); // A=0, B=1, C=2

keywords.description: string - Built-in type for UTF-8 encoded text. Immutable sequence of bytes.
int - Platform-dependent signed integer (32 or 64 bits). Most common integer type.
int8 - 8-bit signed integer (-128 to 127).
int16 - 16-bit signed integer (-32768 to 32767).
int32 - 32-bit signed integer. Alias for rune.
int64 - 64-bit signed integer. Use for very large numbers or timestamps.
uint - Platform-dependent unsigned integer (32 or 64 bits).
uint8 - 8-bit unsigned integer (0 to 255). Alias for byte.
uint16 - 16-bit unsigned integer (0 to 65535).
uint32 - 32-bit unsigned integer.
uint64 - 64-bit unsigned integer.
float32 - 32-bit floating point number.
float64 - 64-bit floating point number. Default for floating point literals.
complex64 - Complex number with float32 real and imaginary parts.
complex128 - Complex number with float64 real and imaginary parts.
bool - Boolean type with values true or false.
byte - Alias for uint8. Commonly used for raw binary data.
rune - Alias for int32. Represents a Unicode code point.
keywords.example: var ch rune = '世'; for _, r := range "hello" { }

keywords.description: map - Built-in hash table type. Maps keys to values.
fallthrough - Transfers control to next case in switch. Rarely used in Go.
keywords.example: switch x { case 1: doSomething(); fallthrough; case 2: doMore() }

keywords.description: goto - Jumps to a labeled statement. Generally discouraged except for specific cases.
print - Formats and writes to standard error. Useful for bootstrapping and debugging.
println - Like print but appends a newline. Writes to standard error.
complex - Constructs a complex number from two floats: complex(realPart, imagPart).
real - Returns the real part of a complex number.
imag - Returns the imaginary part of a complex number.
clear - Deletes all entries from a map, or zeroes all elements of a slice. Added in Go 1.21.
min - Returns the smallest of its arguments. Works with ordered types. Added in Go 1.21.
max - Returns the largest of its arguments. Works with ordered types. Added in Go 1.21.
uintptr - Integer type large enough to hold a pointer value. Used with unsafe package.
any - Alias for interface{}. Accepts any value. Added in Go 1.18.
comparable - Type constraint for types supporting == and !=. Used in generics.
keywords.example: func Index[T comparable](s []T, v T) int { ... }

keywords.description: ~ (tilde) - Underlying type constraint. ~int matches all types whose underlying type is int.
//go:embed - Embeds files/directories into the binary at compile time. Used with embed.FS.
//go:generate - Specifies a command to run with 'go generate'. For code generation.
//go:build - Build constraint tag. Controls when a file is included in the build.
//go:noinline - Prevents the compiler from inlining a function.
//go:nosplit - Prevents stack-split check on function entry. For low-level runtime code.
//go:linkname - Links a local symbol to a symbol in another package. For accessing unexported symbols.
runtime.GOMAXPROCS - Sets the max number of OS threads executing goroutines simultaneously.
Benchmark function prefix - Functions starting with Benchmark are run by 'go test -bench'.
keywords.example: func BenchmarkSort(b *testing.B) { for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ { sort(data) } }

keywords.description: Example function prefix - Testable examples that appear in godoc and are verified by 'go test'.
Fuzz function prefix - Fuzz test entry point. Run with 'go test -fuzz'. Added in Go 1.18.
init - Special function called automatically at package initialization. Runs before main.
_ (blank identifier) - Discards a value. Used to ignore return values or enforce interface compliance.

snippets.description: Main function template - Entry point for Go programs
Function declaration with parameters and return type
For-range loop over slice or map

snippets.content: ${1:ch} := make(chan ${2:type}, ${3:bufferSize})
defer ${1:cleanup}()
defer ${1:file}.Close()

http.HandleFunc("${1:/}", ${2:handler})
log.Fatal(http.ListenAndServe(":${3:8080}", nil))

ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), ${1:5*time.Second})
defer cancel()

snippets.description: Error check with fmt.Errorf wrapping using %w
Check if error matches a target using errors.Is
Custom error type implementing the error interface
errgroup pattern for concurrent tasks with error propagation
Fan-out worker pool with buffered channels
Read-write mutex for concurrent reads, exclusive writes
HTTP client with timeout and request
Write JSON response with status code and content-type
HTTP mux with routes (Go 1.22+ enhanced patterns)
snippets.content:
mux := http.NewServeMux()

mux.HandleFunc("GET /api/${1:items}", ${2:listHandler})
mux.HandleFunc("POST /api/${1:items}", ${3:createHandler})
mux.HandleFunc("GET /api/${1:items}/{id}", ${4:getHandler})
mux.HandleFunc("DELETE /api/${1:items}/{id}", ${5:deleteHandler})

http.ListenAndServe(":${6:8080}", mux)

snippets.description: Parallel subtest using t.Run and t.Parallel
Context with cancel for manual cancellation
Context with deadline for absolute timeout
snippets.content:
deadline := time.Now().Add(${1:5 * time.Second})
ctx, cancel := context.WithDeadline(${2:context.Background()}, deadline)
defer cancel()
${3:}

snippets.description: Context with key-value for request-scoped data
Read file line by line with bufio.Scanner
Create and use a temporary file
Struct with JSON tags and common options
Decode JSON from an io.Reader (HTTP body, file)
Constructor function with options pattern (functional options)
Enum using iota with String() method

snippets.content: var _ ${1:io.Reader} = (*${2:MyType})(nil)

snippets.description: Main with graceful shutdown on OS signal
Compile and use a regular expression
Sort a slice with custom less function
Iterate over a map with sorted keys

items.name: Println
items.description: Print with newline

items.name: Printf
items.description: Formatted print

items.name: Sprintf
items.description: Format to string

items.name: Errorf
items.description: Format error message

items.name: Fprintf
items.description: Formats and writes to an io.Writer

items.name: Fscan
items.description: Reads from an io.Reader, scanning space-delimited values

items.name: Stringer
items.description: Interface with String() string method for custom formatting

items.name: HandleFunc
items.description: Register HTTP handler

items.name: ListenAndServe
items.description: Start HTTP server

items.name: Get
items.description: HTTP GET request

items.name: Post
items.description: HTTP POST request

items.name: ServeMux
items.description: HTTP request multiplexer (router). Enhanced patterns in Go 1.22+

items.name: Client
items.description: HTTP client with configurable timeout, transport, and redirect policy

items.name: StatusOK
items.description: HTTP 200 status code

items.name: StatusNotFound
items.description: HTTP 404 status code

items.name: StatusInternalServerError
items.description: HTTP 500 status code

items.name: MethodGet
items.description: HTTP GET method string

items.name: MethodPost
items.description: HTTP POST method string

items.name: NewRequestWithContext
items.description: Creates an HTTP request with context for cancellation and timeouts

items.name: Marshal
items.description: Encode to JSON

items.name: Unmarshal
items.description: Decode from JSON

items.name: Encoder
items.description: JSON encoder

items.name: Decoder
items.description: JSON decoder

items.name: NewEncoder
items.description: Creates a JSON encoder writing to an io.Writer

items.name: NewDecoder
items.description: Creates a JSON decoder reading from an io.Reader

items.name: RawMessage
items.description: Raw encoded JSON value. Delays decoding or pre-encodes.

items.name: Valid
items.description: Reports whether data is valid JSON encoding

items.name: Background
items.description: Empty context

items.name: WithCancel
items.description: Cancelable context

items.name: WithTimeout
items.description: Context with timeout

items.name: WithDeadline
items.description: Context with deadline

items.name: WithValue
items.description: Returns a context carrying a key-value pair for request-scoped data

items.name: Cause
items.description: Returns the cause of a context cancellation (Go 1.20+)

items.name: AfterFunc
items.description: Registers a function to be called when context is done (Go 1.21+)

items.name: Mutex
items.description: Mutual exclusion lock

items.name: RWMutex
items.description: Reader/writer lock

items.name: WaitGroup
items.description: Wait for goroutines

items.name: Once
items.description: One-time execution

items.name: Map
items.description: Concurrent map safe for use by multiple goroutines without locking

items.name: Pool
items.description: Pool of temporary objects to reduce GC pressure

items.name: Cond
items.description: Condition variable for goroutine coordination (Wait/Signal/Broadcast)

items.name: Open
items.description: Open file

items.name: Create
items.description: Create file

items.name: ReadFile
items.description: Read entire file

items.name: WriteFile
items.description: Write to file

items.name: MkdirAll
items.description: Creates a directory and all parents. Like mkdir -p.

items.name: UserHomeDir
items.description: Returns the current user's home directory

items.name: Executable
items.description: Returns the path of the running executable

items.name: ErrNotExist
items.description: Sentinel error for file/directory not found

items.name: ErrPermission
items.description: Sentinel error for permission denied

items.name: Reader
items.description: Basic read interface

items.name: Writer
items.description: Basic write interface

items.name: Copy
items.description: Copy from reader to writer

items.name: ReadAll
items.description: Reads all bytes from a Reader until EOF

items.name: NopCloser
items.description: Wraps a Reader with a no-op Close method to satisfy ReadCloser

items.name: TeeReader
items.description: Returns a Reader that writes to w what it reads from r

items.name: Pipe
items.description: Creates a synchronous in-memory pipe (PipeReader, PipeWriter)

items.name: MultiReader
items.description: Concatenates multiple Readers into one sequential Reader

items.name: EOF
items.description: Sentinel error returned when no more input is available

items.name: Now
items.description: Current time

items.name: Sleep
items.description: Pause execution

items.name: Duration
items.description: Time duration

items.name: Time
items.description: Point in time

items.name: NewTicker
items.description: Creates a ticker sending the current time on a channel at intervals

items.name: NewTimer
items.description: Creates a timer that fires once after the given duration

items.name: Parse
items.description: Parses a time string using Go's reference time layout

items.name: Since
items.description: Returns duration elapsed since t. Shorthand for time.Now().Sub(t).

items.name: Until
items.description: Returns duration until t. Shorthand for t.Sub(time.Now()).

items.name: RFC3339
items.description: RFC 3339 time format layout: "2006-01-02T15:04:05Z07:00"

items.name: T
items.description: Testing context

items.name: B
items.description: Benchmarking context

items.name: Run
items.description: t.Run runs a subtest with a given name and function

items.name: Parallel
items.description: t.Parallel marks a test to run in parallel with other parallel tests

items.name: Cleanup
items.description: t.Cleanup registers a function to be called when the test finishes

items.name: TempDir
items.description: t.TempDir returns a temporary directory that is cleaned up after test

items.name: Helper
items.description: t.Helper marks the calling function as a test helper for better error output

items.name: New
items.description: Create new error

items.name: Is
items.description: Compare errors

items.name: As
items.description: Type assertion for errors

items.name: Join
items.description: Wraps multiple errors into one. Returns nil if all errors are nil. (Go 1.20+)

items.name: Contains
items.description: Reports whether substr is within s

items.name: HasPrefix
items.description: Tests whether s begins with prefix

items.name: HasSuffix
items.description: Tests whether s ends with suffix

items.name: Join
items.description: Concatenates slice elements with separator

items.name: Split
items.description: Splits s around each instance of sep

items.name: TrimSpace
items.description: Returns s with leading and trailing whitespace removed

items.name: Replace
items.description: Returns s with first n replacements of old with new (-1 for all)

items.name: Builder
items.description: Efficient string builder. Minimizes memory copying.

items.name: NewReader
items.description: Returns a Reader reading from the given string

items.name: Map
items.description: Returns a copy of s with all characters modified by the mapping function

items.name: Cut
items.description: Slices s around the first sep. Returns before, after, found. (Go 1.18+)

items.name: CutPrefix
items.description: Returns s without the prefix and whether it had it (Go 1.20+)

items.name: CutSuffix
items.description: Returns s without the suffix and whether it had it (Go 1.20+)

items.name: Clone
items.description: Returns a fresh copy of s, freeing the underlying memory (Go 1.20+)

items.name: Atoi
items.description: Converts string to int. Shorthand for ParseInt(s, 10, 0).

items.name: Itoa
items.description: Converts int to string. Shorthand for FormatInt(int64(i), 10).

items.name: ParseFloat
items.description: Converts string to float64 with given bit size

items.name: ParseBool
items.description: Converts string to bool. Accepts 1/0/t/f/true/false/etc.

items.name: FormatFloat
items.description: Converts float64 to string with format, precision, and bit size

items.name: FormatInt
items.description: Returns the string representation of i in the given base

items.name: Info
items.description: Log at INFO level with structured key-value pairs

items.name: Warn
items.description: Log at WARN level with structured key-value pairs

items.name: Error
items.description: Log at ERROR level with structured key-value pairs

items.name: Debug
items.description: Log at DEBUG level with structured key-value pairs

items.name: With
items.description: Returns a Logger with additional default attributes

items.name: NewJSONHandler
items.description: Creates a handler that writes JSON-formatted log records

items.name: NewTextHandler
items.description: Creates a handler that writes human-readable log records

items.name: SetDefault
items.description: Sets the default logger for the slog package functions

items.name: Sort
items.description: Sorts a slice of ordered types in ascending order

items.name: SortFunc
items.description: Sorts a slice using a custom comparison function

items.name: BinarySearch
items.description: Searches a sorted slice for a target value

items.name: Contains
items.description: Reports whether v is present in s

items.name: Index
items.description: Returns the index of the first occurrence of v, or -1

items.name: Compact
items.description: Removes consecutive duplicate elements (like Unix uniq)

items.name: Clip
items.description: Removes unused capacity, returning s[:len(s):len(s)]

items.name: Grow
items.description: Increases the capacity by at least n elements

items.name: Delete
items.description: Removes elements s[i:j] and returns the modified slice

items.name: Replace
items.description: Replaces elements s[i:j] with the given values

items.name: Reverse
items.description: Reverses the elements of the slice in place

items.name: Equal
items.description: Reports whether two slices are equal element by element

items.name: Keys
items.description: Returns the keys of the map in an indeterminate order

items.name: Values
items.description: Returns the values of the map in an indeterminate order

items.name: Clone
items.description: Returns a shallow copy of the map

items.name: Copy
items.description: Copies all key-value pairs from src to dst

items.name: DeleteFunc
items.description: Deletes entries for which the function returns true

items.name: Equal
items.description: Reports whether two maps contain the same key-value pairs

items.name: MustCompile
items.description: Compiles a regex and panics on error. For package-level vars.

items.name: Compile
items.description: Compiles a regex and returns an error if invalid

items.name: MatchString
items.description: Reports whether the string contains any match of the pattern

items.name: FindString
items.description: Returns the leftmost match of the regex in the string

items.name: FindStringSubmatch
items.description: Returns a slice of the match and captured groups

items.name: ReplaceAllString
items.description: Returns string with all matches replaced by repl

items.name: Slice
items.description: Sorts a slice given a less function. Not stable.

items.name: SliceStable
items.description: Sorts a slice preserving original order of equal elements

items.name: Search
items.description: Binary search returning the smallest index i where f(i) is true

items.name: Strings
items.description: Sorts a slice of strings in ascending order

items.name: Ints
items.description: Sorts a slice of ints in ascending order

items.name: Join
items.description: Joins path elements with the OS separator, cleaning the result

items.name: Dir
items.description: Returns all but the last element of path (parent directory)

items.name: Base
items.description: Returns the last element of path (filename)

items.name: Ext
items.description: Returns the file extension including the dot

items.name: Abs
items.description: Returns an absolute path representation

items.name: Walk
items.description: Walks the file tree rooted at root, calling fn for each file

items.name: WalkDir
items.description: Like Walk but uses fs.DirEntry for efficiency (Go 1.16+)

items.name: Glob
items.description: Returns names of files matching the shell pattern

items.name: Rel
items.description: Returns a relative path from basepath to targpath

items.name: NewScanner
items.description: Returns a Scanner reading from r. Splits on lines by default.

items.name: NewReader
items.description: Returns a buffered Reader with default buffer size

items.name: NewWriter
items.description: Returns a buffered Writer with default buffer size

items.name: Scanner
items.description: Reads input and splits into tokens (lines, words, bytes, runes)

items.name: ScanLines
items.description: Split function for Scanner that returns each line of text

items.name: ScanWords
items.description: Split function for Scanner that returns each space-delimited word

items.name: FS
items.description: Read-only collection of files embedded at compile time

items.name: ReadFile
items.description: Reads and returns the content of the named file from the FS

items.name: ReadDir
items.description: Reads and returns the directory entries of the named directory

items.name: Open
items.description: Opens a database with the given driver name and DSN

items.name: DB
items.description: Database handle. Safe for concurrent use. Manages connection pool.

items.name: Tx
items.description: Database transaction. Must be committed or rolled back.

items.name: Row
items.description: Result of QueryRow. Call Scan to read the selected row.

items.name: Rows
items.description: Result of Query. Iterate with Next() and scan each row.

items.name: NullString
items.description: Represents a string that may be NULL in the database

items.name: NullInt64
items.description: Represents an int64 that may be NULL in the database

items.name: Parse
items.description: Parses a raw URL string into a URL structure

items.name: URL
items.description: Represents a parsed URL with scheme, host, path, query, etc.

items.name: Values
items.description: URL query parameters (map[string][]string) with Get/Set/Add/Encode

items.name: QueryEscape
items.description: Escapes a string for safe URL query parameter inclusion

items.name: PathEscape
items.description: Escapes a string for safe URL path segment inclusion

items.name: Buffer
items.description: Variable-size byte buffer with Read and Write methods

items.name: NewBuffer
items.description: Creates a Buffer initialized with the given byte slice

items.name: Contains
items.description: Reports whether subslice is within b

items.name: Join
items.description: Concatenates byte slices with separator

items.name: Split
items.description: Splits byte slice around each instance of sep

items.name: TrimSpace
items.description: Returns byte slice with leading and trailing whitespace removed
